data.narrative:
uAvionix is a Series B-stage aerospace company that has raised $55M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public Space & Aerospace names (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with direct same-stage comparables including Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression methodology, using the $55M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $104M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $400M implied valuation, with the sample ranging from $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable given the absence of target revenue data.

Synthesizing the two methods produces a triangulated range of $89M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $252M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity within the peer set—particularly the $1.3B outlier (Aalyria) versus the $274M floor (Sift). Key uncertainties include uAvionix's current revenue run-rate, unit economics, and the degree to which its technology differentiation aligns with high-valuation comparables versus mid-range peers. The stage-progression method anchors toward the lower bound, while peer medians support the mid-to-upper range.
